components.name: Header/Navbar
components.description: Fixed top navigation bar present on every page. Contains the Prometheus logo, primary route links, and right-side utility ActionIcons. Background is a dark slate that does not change between light and dark themes.
components.subtitle: Global app shell navigation

props.name: activeRoute
props.description: Current route path — highlights the matching nav link.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Full navbar with logo, route links (Alerts, Graph, Status, Help), and utility icons (theme toggle, settings, docs).
variants.mock_html:
🔥 Prometheus
GraphAlertsStatusHelp

dos_and_donts.text: Keep the navbar background rgb(65,73,81) identical in light and dark themes — it is a product-brand anchor.
Use Tabler Icons for all ActionIcons to stay consistent with the rest of the icon set.
Don't change the navbar background based on color scheme — it breaks the brand identity.
Don't add more than 4 primary route links without a dropdown or secondary nav.

accessibility.aria_labels: Theme toggle ActionIcon uses aria-label that flips between 'Switch to light theme' / 'Switch to dark theme'.
accessibility.keyboard: Tab through nav links and ActionIcons. Active route link should have aria-current='page'.
accessibility.contrast: White text on rgb(65,73,81) slate passes WCAG AA (contrast ~5.1:1).

components.code_example:
<AppShell header={{ height: 56 }}>
  <AppShell.Header style={{ background: 'rgb(65,73,81)' }}>
    <Group h="100%" px="md" justify="space-between">
      <Text c="white" fw={700}>Prometheus</Text>
      <Group gap="xs">
        <NavLink label="Graph" href="/query" />
        <NavLink label="Alerts" href="/alerts" />
      </Group>
      <Group gap="xs">
        <ActionIcon aria-label="Switch to dark theme" variant="transparent" color="white">
          <IconSun size={18} />
        </ActionIcon>
      </Group>
    </Group>
  </AppShell.Header>
</AppShell>

components.name: Mantine Card
components.description: Primary content container used on 7 of 10 pages. Wraps all major content sections with a consistent white/dark background, 1px border, 4px corner radius, and a subtle box shadow.
components.subtitle: Content section container

variants.name: default
variants.description: Standard content card with border and shadow.

dos_and_donts.text: Use Mantine Card as the single wrapping container for any page-level content section.
Maintain 16px internal padding consistently — do not override with tighter values.
Don't nest cards inside cards — use Accordion or Table rows for sub-grouping instead.
Don't use the card border-radius token (4px) for pill/badge shapes — use the pill token (1000px).

accessibility.contrast: Card bg passes WCAG AA for body text in both light (black on white) and dark (rgb(201,201,201) on rgb(46,46,46)).

components.name: Card Title with Icon
components.description: Card header variant that places a semantically relevant Tabler icon immediately before the title text. Used on /status (Build, Runtime) and /alertmanager-discovery (Active, Dropped).
components.subtitle: Titled card header with leading icon

props.name: icon
props.description: Tabler icon component matching the card's semantic content.

variants.name: with-icon
variants.description: Title row with leading Tabler icon (18px) followed by heading text.

dos_and_donts.text: Choose an icon that semantically relates to the card content (database → build, bell → alerts).
Apply this pattern consistently to all cards that currently have it — and consider extending it to cards without icons (INC-001).
Don't use decorative or random icons — every icon should communicate something about the card's subject.
Don't mix icon-title cards and plain-title cards on the same page without visual justification.

accessibility.aria_labels: Icon should be aria-hidden='true' — the title text carries the accessible name.
accessibility.screen_reader: Screen readers should read the title only; hide icon with aria-hidden.

components.code_example:
<Group gap="xs" mb="xs">
  <IconDatabase size={18} aria-hidden />
  <Title order={4}>Build Information</Title>
</Group>

components.name: Card Title without Icon
components.description: Card header variant that renders only the title text with no leading icon. Used on /tsdb-status and /flags. Visually lighter than the icon variant and creates an inconsistency with icon-title cards elsewhere.
components.subtitle: Plain text card header

variants.name: plain
variants.description: Title rendered as heading text with no leading icon.

dos_and_donts.text: Use plain titles for cards where no icon adds semantic value.
Don't mix icon and plain card titles on the same page — pick one style and apply it consistently (see INC-001).

components.name: Mantine Alert Info Callout
components.description: Shared empty-state pattern rendered as a Mantine Alert in 'info' color. Appears on /alerts, /rules, and /alertmanager-discovery when there is no data to display. Provides a subtle blue tint in both themes.
components.subtitle: Empty-state info alert

props.name: color
props.description: Mantine color — always 'blue' in this app.

variants.name: info
variants.description: Blue-tinted informational callout used as empty state.

dos_and_donts.text: Use this pattern consistently for all empty states across the app.
Include a meaningful message explaining why the list is empty (e.g. 'No rules found' not just 'Empty').
Don't use this for error states — use the error badge/status pattern instead.
Don't add a border to the callout — it does not have one in the current implementation.

accessibility.aria_labels: Mantine Alert renders with role='alert' by default.
accessibility.contrast: Light: rgb(24,100,171) on rgba(34,139,230,0.1) ≈ 4.7:1 — passes AA. Dark: rgb(116,192,252) on rgba(34,139,230,0.15) — verify contrast.
accessibility.screen_reader: role='alert' causes screen readers to announce the content. Use only for genuinely important empty states.

components.code_example:
<Alert color="blue" icon={<IconInfoCircle />}>
  No alerting rules found.
</Alert>

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary call-to-action button in the app — the 'Execute' button on the PromQL query page. Filled blue background with white text, 14px semibold, 4px radius.
components.subtitle: Primary filled action button

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows loading spinner while query executes.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Filled primary blue button.

dos_and_donts.text: Use this variant only for the single primary action per page/form.
Show a loading state during async operations (Mantine Button loading prop).
Don't use more than one primary button per surface — it dilutes the hierarchy.
Don't use a different blue — only rgb(34,139,230) light / rgb(25,113,194) dark.

accessibility.keyboard: Focusable via Tab, activatable via Enter/Space.
accessibility.contrast: White on rgb(34,139,230) = ~4.5:1 — passes WCAG AA.

components.name: Button Variants Group
components.description: The dense toolbar on the PromQL query page containing nine visually distinct button variants: Execute (primary filled), metrics explorer (subtle), tree-view toggle, result tabs (segmented control), timestamp toggle, history dropdown, and add-panel button.
components.subtitle: Multi-variant query toolbar

variants.name: subtle
variants.description: Subtle text-only button used for secondary actions (metrics explorer, tree-view toggle).

variants.name: segmented-control
variants.description: Tab-style segmented control for result view (Graph / Table / Explain).

variants.name: action-icon
variants.description: Icon-only ActionIcon for compact secondary actions (history, add panel).

dos_and_donts.text: Group related controls visually using gaps and Mantine Group component.
Reserve the filled/primary variant for the single most important action.
Don't add more primary-filled buttons to this toolbar — the Execute button is the only call-to-action.
Don't replicate this dense toolbar pattern elsewhere — it is specific to the query surface.

accessibility.aria_labels: Each ActionIcon needs an explicit aria-label. SegmentedControl should use role='tablist' / role='tab'.
accessibility.keyboard: All controls reachable via Tab. SegmentedControl navigable with arrow keys.

components.code_example:
<Group gap="xs">
  <Button variant="filled" color="blue">Execute</Button>
  <Button variant="subtle">Metrics Explorer</Button>
  <SegmentedControl data={['Graph', 'Table', 'Explain']} />
  <ActionIcon aria-label="History" variant="subtle">
    <IconHistory size={18} />
  </ActionIcon>
</Group>

components.name: Filter Input (Pills)
components.description: Multi-filter toolbar built with Mantine PillsInput that lets users add multiple chip-style labeled filters. Used across four pages for label-oriented filtering. Placeholder text is shared verbatim between /alerts and /rules.
components.subtitle: Multi-chip filter toolbar

props.name: placeholder
props.default: Filter by rule name or labels
props.description: Input placeholder text shown when no filters are active.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Empty filter bar with placeholder.

dos_and_donts.text: Use this for list pages where users need to filter by multiple label dimensions simultaneously.
Keep placeholder text descriptive of what can be filtered (e.g. 'Filter by rule name or labels').
Don't use this where a simple text match is sufficient — consider the single-field filter instead (INC-004).
Don't use the same placeholder on different pages if the filterable fields differ.

accessibility.aria_labels: PillsInput should have an associated label element.
accessibility.keyboard: Tab to enter input, Enter/comma to commit chip, Backspace to remove last chip.
accessibility.screen_reader: Each pill should be announced when added/removed.

components.code_example:
<PillsInput label="Filter">
  <Pill.Group>
    {values.map(v => <Pill key={v} withRemoveButton>{v}</Pill>)}
    <PillsInput.Field placeholder="Filter by rule name or labels" />
  </Pill.Group>
</PillsInput>

components.name: Filter Input (Single Field)
components.description: Simple full-width Mantine TextInput with a leading search icon affix. Used only on /flags for substring filtering. Simpler interaction model than the Pills variant — no chip creation, just text match.
components.subtitle: Single-field text filter

props.name: placeholder
props.default: Filter by flag name or value

variants.name: default
variants.description: Full-width text input with search icon prefix.
variants.mock_html: 🔍

dos_and_donts.text: Use for simple substring filtering when no label-structured data is involved.
Don't use this on pages that filter by structured labels — use the Pills variant instead (INC-004).
Don't omit the search icon prefix — it signals the input is a filter, not a form field.

accessibility.aria_labels: Include a visible label or aria-label='Filter flags'.

components.code_example:
<TextInput
  placeholder="Filter by flag name or value"
  leftSection={<IconSearch size={18} />}
/>

components.name: Health Status Badge
components.description: Green/red pill badge shown on each scrape target to indicate health status ('UP' or 'DOWN'). Fully rounded (pill radius). Light/dark swap the fg and bg values. Note: DOM contains lowercase 'up'/'down' but CSS uppercases via textTransform — see INC-007.
components.subtitle: Target health status indicator

props.name: status
props.description: Health state of the scrape target.

variants.name: ok
variants.description: Green pill for healthy targets. DOM: 'up', CSS renders: 'UP'.

variants.name: error
variants.description: Red pill for unhealthy targets. DOM: 'down', CSS renders: 'DOWN'.

dos_and_donts.text: Author the DOM text in uppercase ('UP', 'DOWN') to match visual output — removes reliance on textTransform (INC-007).
Use the ok/error status tokens for background and text — never hard-code the hex values.
Don't use textTransform to uppercase lowercase source text — it breaks copy-paste and screen-reader output (INC-007).
Don't add a third state (e.g. 'UNKNOWN') without also adding the corresponding token pair.

accessibility.aria_labels: Consider aria-label='Health: UP' so status is explicit to screen readers.
accessibility.contrast: Light: rgb(43,138,62) on rgb(211,249,216) ≈ 4.1:1 — marginally passes AA for large text. Dark: rgb(211,249,216) on rgb(43,138,62) — similar.
accessibility.screen_reader: Current lowercase DOM text 'up'/'down' may be announced differently than the visible 'UP'/'DOWN' — see INC-007.

components.name: Label Badge
components.description: Grey pill used to render scrape-target key=value labels. Preserves original case (textTransform: none). High density — up to 200 instances on the targets page. Shares visual appearance with Stats Badge but has different casing rules.
components.subtitle: Key=value label chip

variants.name: default
variants.description: Grey pill preserving label case.

dos_and_donts.text: Always preserve label case exactly — label names and values are case-sensitive in Prometheus.
Don't apply textTransform: uppercase to label badges — it conflicts with Prometheus label semantics (INC-003).
Don't confuse with Stats Badge — both look similar but have different casing rules.

accessibility.contrast: Dark: rgb(173,181,189) on rgb(52,58,64) — check contrast for small text (12px).

components.name: Stats Badge
components.description: Grey pill used for scrape-pool statistics (e.g. ENDPOINTS, HEALTHY). CSS uppercases text regardless of authored case. Visually identical to Label Badge, but the casing rule diverges — see INC-003.
components.subtitle: Scrape-pool statistics chip

props.name: stat
props.description: Statistic label, e.g. 'endpoints' (rendered as 'ENDPOINTS' by CSS).

variants.name: default
variants.description: Grey pill with CSS-uppercased stat name.

dos_and_donts.text: Author stat names in uppercase directly in the DOM rather than relying on CSS textTransform (resolves INC-003 partially).
Don't use the same visual style as Label Badge without a clear visual differentiator — users cannot tell which rule applies to which badge type.

accessibility.screen_reader: CSS textTransform: uppercase may cause screen readers to spell out individual letters. Author uppercase text in DOM.

components.name: Mantine Accordion
components.description: Collapsible sections used on /targets to group scrape pools. Each accordion header shows the pool name plus Stats Badges summarizing endpoint counts. Body reveals the target table for that pool.
components.subtitle: Collapsible scrape-pool group

variants.name: default
variants.description: Expandable pool group with header stats and body content.
variants.mock_html:
▶prometheus/0ENDPOINTSHEALTHY
Target table content

dos_and_donts.text: Use accordion to group logically related rows that users may want to collapse independently.
Put summary badges in the accordion header so users can scan without expanding.
Don't use accordion for fewer than 3 groups — flat list is simpler to scan.

accessibility.aria_labels: Mantine Accordion uses proper aria-expanded, aria-controls on the trigger button.
accessibility.keyboard: Tab to header, Enter/Space to toggle, arrow keys between items.
accessibility.screen_reader: Accordion state (expanded/collapsed) is communicated via aria-expanded.

components.code_example:
<Accordion>
  <Accordion.Item value="prometheus/0">
    <Accordion.Control>
      <Group gap="xs">
        prometheus/0
        <Badge>3 ENDPOINTS</Badge>
      </Group>
    </Accordion.Control>
    <Accordion.Panel>{/* target table */}</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion>

components.name: Sortable Table
components.description: Mantine table with clickable column headers for sorting. Uses a stacked chevron-up + chevron-down icon pair to signal sortability. Only /flags uses this — other tables are static (see INC-005).
components.subtitle: Column-sortable data table

variants.name: default
variants.description: Two-column table with sortable Flag and Value headers.

dos_and_donts.text: Show the stacked chevron icon on every sortable column header so users know they can click.
Highlight the currently active sort column header.
Don't make tables sortable only in some pages — apply sort consistently where it adds value (INC-005).

accessibility.keyboard: Sort headers should be button elements or have tabindex='0' and respond to Enter.

components.code_example: toggleSort('flag')} style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }} aria-sort={sortField === 'flag' ? sortDirection : 'none'} > Flag

components.name: Key-Value Table
components.description: 2-column definition-list table with bold key column and regular value column, 1px row dividers. Used for build info, runtime info, and TSDB head status. Static — no sort controls.
components.subtitle: Property definition table

variants.name: default
variants.description: 2-column key/value table with dividers.

dos_and_donts.text: Use this pattern for static metadata — build info, config values, system properties.
Show status values like 'Successful' as colored pills rather than plain text (INC-006).
Don't use this for large data sets — use Sortable Table instead.
Don't render status text as plain prose when a health badge conveys the same information more clearly (INC-006).

accessibility.aria_labels: Consider using a dl/dt/dd structure for purely definition-list content.

components.name: Discovered/Target Labels Layout
components.description: Two-column side-by-side layout on /service-discovery. Left column shows 'Discovered Labels' and right column shows 'Target Labels' for each discovered endpoint, both rendered as groups of Label Badges.
components.subtitle: Side-by-side label comparison layout

variants.name: default
variants.description: Two-column label panel with section heading and bottom divider.
variants.mock_html:
Discovered Labels
__address__=localhost:9090
Target Labels
instance=localhost:9090

dos_and_donts.text: Always show both columns even if one is empty — the comparison is the UX.
Use the same Label Badge component for both columns.
Don't collapse to a single column on non-mobile viewports — the side-by-side comparison is the point.

accessibility.aria_labels: Section headings should be heading elements (h3/h4) for screen reader navigation.

components.code_example:
<Grid>
  <Grid.Col span={6}>
    <Title order={5} mb="xs" pb="xs" style={{ borderBottom: '1px solid' }}>Discovered Labels</Title>
    <Group gap={4}>
      {Object.entries(discoveredLabels).map(([k, v]) => <Badge key={k}>{k}={v}</Badge>)}
    </Group>
  </Grid.Col>
  <Grid.Col span={6}>
    <Title order={5} mb="xs" pb="xs" style={{ borderBottom: '1px solid' }}>Target Labels</Title>
    <Group gap={4}>
      {Object.entries(targetLabels).map(([k, v]) => <Badge key={k}>{k}={v}</Badge>)}
    </Group>
  </Grid.Col>
</Grid>

components.name: YAML Code Block
components.description: Full config rendered as a <pre><code class='language-yaml'> block with highlight.js syntax highlighting inside a Mantine Card. Font is browser-default 'monospace' with no explicit stack (INC-009). Syntax token colors swap roles between light and dark themes (INC-008).
components.subtitle: Syntax-highlighted configuration display

variants.name: default
variants.description: Highlight.js YAML-highlighted code block in a card.

dos_and_donts.text: Declare an explicit monospace font stack (e.g. 'ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, monospace') to ensure consistent rendering across OS (fixes INC-009).
Keep hljs-attr and hljs-string in the same hue families across light and dark to avoid color-role swaps (fixes INC-008).
Don't use 'monospace' as the sole font-family value — it resolves to different fonts on macOS, Windows, and Linux.
Don't let highlight.js token colors swap semantic roles (keys ↔ strings) between themes.

accessibility.contrast: Light hljs-attr rgb(92,148,13) on white — check contrast for WCAG AA. Dark hljs-attr rgb(165,216,255) on dark bg — verify.
accessibility.screen_reader: Pre blocks should have role='region' with an aria-label if the content is important.

components.name: Copy Button
components.description: Mantine ActionIcon with a clipboard icon, positioned top-right of the config card on /config. Part of the ActionIcon primitive family also used in the navbar (theme toggle, settings, docs).
components.subtitle: Copy-to-clipboard action icon

variants.name: default
variants.description: Clipboard icon ActionIcon, idle state.

variants.name: copied
variants.description: Checkmark feedback state after successful copy.

dos_and_donts.text: Show a brief 'copied' visual feedback state (checkmark icon, green color) for 2s after copy.
Use Mantine CopyButton wrapper to handle clipboard API and feedback state.
Don't show a toast/notification for a copy action — the in-button feedback is sufficient.
Don't place the copy button inside the scrollable pre block — position it above the content for constant visibility.

accessibility.aria_labels: Use aria-label='Copy configuration' on the ActionIcon.
accessibility.keyboard: Focusable via Tab, activatable via Enter/Space.
accessibility.screen_reader: Update aria-label to 'Copied!' during feedback state so screen readers announce the result.

components.code_example:
<CopyButton value={configYaml} timeout={2000}>
  {({ copied, copy }) => (
    <ActionIcon
      aria-label={copied ? 'Copied!' : 'Copy configuration'}
      color={copied ? 'green' : 'blue'}
      variant="subtle"
      onClick={copy}
    >
      {copied ? <IconCheck size={18} /> : <IconClipboard size={18} />}
    </ActionIcon>
  )}
</CopyButton>

action_items.title: Standardize card title icon usage across all pages
action_items.description: INC-001: /status and /alertmanager-discovery use leading Tabler icons in card titles; /tsdb-status and /flags do not. Pick one style and apply consistently. Recommendation: add icons to /tsdb-status (chart-bar for TSDB, flag for Flags page) to match the more expressive pattern.

before_after.after_html: <Group gap="xs" mb="xs"><IconChartBar size={18} aria-hidden /><Title order={4}>TSDB Head Status</Title></Group>
before_after.diff:
+ import { IconChartBar } from '@tabler/icons-react';
- <Title order={4}>TSDB Head Status</Title>
+ <Group gap="xs" mb="xs">
+   <IconChartBar size={18} aria-hidden />
+   <Title order={4}>TSDB Head Status</Title>
+ </Group>

action_items.github_issue_title: fix: add missing card title icons on /tsdb-status and /flags

action_items.title: Use monospace font for all code-like identifiers
action_items.description: INC-002: /flags wraps flag names in <code> tags (monospace) but /tsdb-status renders equivalent identifiers (__name__, label=value) as plain td text in sans-serif. Wrap identifiers in <code> or apply font-family: monospace via a utility class.
action_items.github_issue_title: fix: render metric/label identifiers in monospace on /tsdb-status

action_items.title: Align text-casing rules between Label Badge and Stats Badge
action_items.description: INC-003: _labelBadge has textTransform: none; _statsBadge has textTransform: uppercase — both share the same visual styling. Recommendation: author stats badge content in uppercase directly in the DOM and remove the CSS rule, matching how /targets health badges should also be fixed.

before_after.diff:
- .statsBadge { text-transform: uppercase; }
// author uppercase text in source instead

action_items.github_issue_title: fix: author stats badge text in uppercase; remove CSS text-transform

action_items.title: Unify filter input pattern across all list pages
action_items.description: INC-004: /alerts, /rules, /targets, /service-discovery use PillsInput; /flags uses a plain TextInput. Recommendation: migrate /flags to PillsInput for consistency, or document the distinction with clear UX rationale. Flags data (name=value) naturally fits label-style filtering.

before_after.before_html: <TextInput placeholder="Filter by flag name or value" leftSection={<IconSearch />} />
before_after.after_html: <PillsInput><Pill.Group><PillsInput.Field placeholder="Filter by flag name or value" /></Pill.Group></PillsInput>

action_items.github_issue_title: feat: migrate /flags filter to PillsInput for consistency with other list pages

action_items.title: Add sort affordance to Key-Value tables on /status and /tsdb-status
action_items.description: INC-005: Only /flags has a sortable table. /status and /tsdb-status have static Key-Value tables. Consider adding optional sort to the top-labels breakdown table on /tsdb-status which lists label names by count — sorting descending by count is the most useful default.
action_items.github_issue_title: feat: make top-labels table on /tsdb-status sortable

action_items.title: Render Configuration Reload status as a health badge
action_items.description: INC-006: 'Configuration Reload: Successful' on /status is plain body text. Consistent with the rest of the app's status rendering, this should be a colored pill (green for Successful, red for Failed). Re-use the Health Status Badge tokens.
action_items.github_issue_title: fix: render config reload status as colored badge on /status

action_items.title: Author health badge text in uppercase in the DOM
action_items.description: INC-007: DOM contains 'up'/'down' (lowercase); CSS textTransform: uppercase renders 'UP'/'DOWN'. Browser find-in-page for 'UP' returns zero matches. Screen readers may announce the lowercase form. Fix: author 'UP'/'DOWN' in source and remove the CSS textTransform rule.

before_after.diff:
- .healthOk { text-transform: uppercase; }
+ /* removed text-transform */
- children = status  // 'up' | 'down'
+ children = status.toUpperCase()  // 'UP' | 'DOWN'

action_items.github_issue_title: fix(a11y): author health badge text in uppercase; remove CSS text-transform

action_items.title: Fix highlight.js token color role swap between themes
action_items.description: INC-008: hljs-attr is olive-green in light but light-blue in dark; hljs-string is blue in light but green in dark — hue roles are swapped. Fix by providing a custom highlight.js theme where attr and string consistently map to the same hue family across both modes.

before_after.before_html: /* Light: attr=green, string=blue. Dark: attr=blue, string=green (swapped) */
before_after.after_html: /* Light: attr=green, string=blue. Dark: attr=green-light, string=blue-light (consistent hue families) */
before_after.diff:
[data-mantine-color-scheme='dark'] .hljs-attr { color: rgb(130,210,100); }  /* green family */
[data-mantine-color-scheme='dark'] .hljs-string { color: rgb(116,192,252); }  /* blue family */

action_items.github_issue_title: fix: keep hljs-attr/hljs-string in consistent hue families across light and dark themes

action_items.title: Add explicit monospace font stack to code and pre elements
action_items.description: INC-009: <pre> on /config and <code> on /flags both use the browser default 'monospace', which resolves differently on macOS, Windows, and Linux. Add an explicit stack: 'ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", "Courier New", monospace'.

before_after.after_html: pre, code { font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', 'Courier New', monospace; }

action_items.github_issue_title: fix: declare explicit monospace font stack for pre and code elements
